documents.content: Teff (ጤፍ) typically needs 50–80 kg/ha DAP at planting. Apply urea top-dressing 40–50 kg/ha about 30 days after emergence. Adjust with soil test when available.
Teff rust shows yellow-orange pustules on leaves. At first symptoms, consider Mancozeb 80% WP per local extension label; confirm diagnosis with extension if unsure.
Wheat often uses 100–120 kg/ha NPS at planting and ~50 kg/ha urea at tillering. Split applications reduce lodging risk on fertile soils.
Maize: common spacing ~75 cm between rows and 25–30 cm within row depending on variety. Plant at reliable soil moisture; follow recommended seed rate on bag.
Irrigate based on crop stage and soil moisture. Avoid waterlogging; many highland cereals are sensitive to poor drainage.
Harvest teff when grains are hard and moisture is appropriate for storage. Dry grain safely before storage to reduce mold.
